name: Sagolath
other_abilities: anchoring (DC 25), death throes, living starship, poisonous injection, pull prey, void adaptation

special_abilities.name: Anchoring (Ex)
special_abilities.description: A sagolath’s hooked tentacles have the anchoring weapon special property. The DC to escape a sagolath’s hooked tentacles increases by 1 for each additional tentacle anchored to the target (up to a maximum DC of 28).

special_abilities.name: Living Starship (Ex)
special_abilities.description:
A sagolath is so immense that it functions as a starship (and thus engages only in starship combat). It has no crew, but it can take engineer, gunner, pilot, and science officer actions (one of each, in the appropriate phase) using the bonuses, level, and ranks listed above. Modifiers for its size, speed, and maneuverability have already been factored into its statistics. Use the following table to determine the effects when a sagolath takes critical damage.
D%
System
Effect
1–30
Tentacles
Randomly determine one arc containing tentacles; condition applies to gunner actions using weapons in that arc. Additionally, the DC to escape from a sagolath’s anchored tentacles is reduced by 1 for 3 rounds.
31–60
Hydrogen Propulsion
Condition applies to all pilot actions.
61–90
Maw
The sagolath can’t use its maw for 3 rounds.
91–100
Brain
During the next round, each of the sagolath’s attempted actions has a 25% chance of failure.

special_abilities.name: Poisonous Injection (Ex)
special_abilities.description: A sagolath’s hooked tentacles inject a powerful poisonous gas into anything it strikes. If the sagolath deals Hull Point damage to an enemy ship, it releases this poison into the ship. At the end of the current gunnery phase, all creatures on the affected ship must attempt a DC 17 Fortitude saving throw or take a –2 penalty on all starship action skill checks during the following round. This is a poison effect.

special_abilities.name: Pull Prey (Ex)
special_abilities.description: During the gunnery phase, a sagolath can pull a ship it has anchored with one of its hooked tentacles closer with a special gunnery action. The sagolath attempts a gunnery check against the anchored ship’s AC. On a success, the ship is dragged 1 hex closer to the sagolath.

description: A sagolath is a massive aberration that lives deep within the atmosphere of a gas giant. It has a pulsating, ovular body, a forward-facing maw filled with threshing teeth, and a corona of grasping hooked tentacles capable of digging into starship hulls with ease. These tentacles inject a poisonous gas into the bloodstreams of larger prey or starship interiors, which weakens biological organisms. A sagolath propels itself by expelling biologically warmed hydrogen.

Special Abilities
Anchoring (Ex)

Living Starship (Ex)
A sagolath is so immense that it functions as a starship (and thus engages only in starship combat). It has no crew, but it can take engineer, gunner, pilot, and science officer actions (one of each, in the appropriate phase) using the bonuses, level, and ranks listed above. Modifiers for its size, speed, and maneuverability have already been factored into its statistics. Use the following table to determine the effects when a sagolath takes critical damage.

Poisonous Injection (Ex)
A sagolath’s hooked tentacles inject a powerful poisonous gas into anything it strikes. If the sagolath deals Hull Point damage to an enemy ship, it releases this poison into the ship. At the end of the current gunnery phase, all creatures on the affected ship must attempt a DC 17 Fortitude saving throw or take a –2 penalty on all starship action skill checks during the following round. This is a poison effect.
Pull Prey (Ex)
During the gunnery phase, a sagolath can pull a ship it has anchored with one of its hooked tentacles closer with a special gunnery action. The sagolath attempts a gunnery check against the anchored ship’s AC. On a success, the ship is dragged 1 hex closer to the sagolath.
Description
A sagolath is a massive aberration that lives deep within the atmosphere of a gas giant. It has a pulsating, ovular body, a forward-facing maw filled with threshing teeth, and a corona of grasping hooked tentacles capable of digging into starship hulls with ease. These tentacles inject a poisonous gas into the bloodstreams of larger prey or starship interiors, which weakens biological organisms. A sagolath propels itself by expelling biologically warmed hydrogen.
